case.summary: Account 80318BAF0 exhibits a fan-in pattern with 38 inbound transactions from 36 distinct senders in 8 days, concentrated in a burst on 2022-09-09 (35+ txns in 2 hours), all one-off relationships. The account retains $76.5k net (inbound $90.9k, outbound only $14.4k in 3 txns). The single meaningful outflow ($3.5k on Sept 4) goes to account 803189520, which itself is mule-shaped (collect_ratio=17.5, passthrough=0.0, 97% new inbound counterparties). While the subject account does not exhibit rapid pass-through behavior (Q2=NO), the combination of all-new inbound relationships (Q1=YES), a suspicious secondary collector as its outbound recipient (Q3=UNCLEAR), and unnatural burst timing raises moderate concern for a secondary collection layer in a network. Lacks a COMPLETED traced structure, but neighbors are mule-shaped. Legitimate counterfactual: merchant/payment aggregator collecting from many small agents.

evidence.label: New relationships
evidence.value: 36 of 37 inbound counterparties are brand-new (first contact on 2022-09-09). Outbound: one new counterparty (803189520, first contact 2022-09-04). No repeating relationships in 8-day account history.

evidence.label: Burst timing
evidence.value: 35+ transactions in 2-hour window on 2022-09-09 (11:30–11:54 UTC), preceded by sparse activity. Coordinated and unnatural timing suggests network coordination.

evidence.label: Neighbor shape: 803189520
evidence.value: The single outflow recipient (803189520) has collect_ratio=17.5, passthrough=0.0, new_sender_share_24h=97%. Exhibits mule characteristics: heavy inbound collection, minimal outbound, all-new sources, zero pass-through.

evidence.label: No pass-through behavior
evidence.value: Account retains $76.5k of $90.9k inbound; outbound is $14.4k. Pass-through ratio = 0.05. Money is kept and mixed, not forwarded intact. Contradicts mule/pass-through typology.

evidence.label: Real business markers
evidence.value: Entity 'Partnership #6764', legitimate bank, diverse payment formats (ACH, Credit Card, Reinvestment), both inbound and outbound activity, multiple sibling accounts. Consistent with a real registered entity.

evidence.label: Account age and profile
evidence.value: Account first seen 2022-09-01, only 8 days old at alert. New account behavior may be normal for startup businesses or merchant platforms. Not inherently suspicious.

evidence.label: No completed trace structure
evidence.value: Trace-funds shows fan-in to 80318BAF0, one onward flow to 803189520 (which sends small amount back in alert txn). No cycle, no stack, no scatter-gather chain verified. Structure is incomplete and ambiguous.

evidence.label: Modest outflow amounts
evidence.value: Total outflow $14.4k across 3 txns over 8 days. Single significant outflow $3.5k on Sept 4. Not characteristic of high-velocity layering; amounts are retail-scale.

case.summary: Account 8010D4440 displays a high-confidence GATHER-SCATTER money laundering structure. The account aggregates inbound payments from 15 brand-new, single-contact senders (FAN-IN) and forwards them to 2-3 downstream hub accounts at high ratios (0.79–0.93) and short lags (17–43 hours). All inbound counterparties are one-hit relationships, and trace analysis reveals mule-shaped upstream senders (high spray ratios, all-new counterparties) feeding into the account. The account is only 8 days old and operates under a generic "Sole Proprietorship" shell with 9 sibling accounts. This is consistent with a temporary mule network established to layer stolen funds through a structured network.

evidence.label: High-ratio pass-through on alerted transaction
evidence.value: Inbound from 8048E30F0 ($17,498.02, 2022-09-05 11:20) flows to 80CD41030 as $18,721.33 (2022-09-07 06:16); ratio=0.935, lag=42.93 hours. Similarly, 8066C7E10 ($16,614.28) → 80CD41030 ($18,721.33) at ratio=0.887, lag=38.77 hours. Multiple sources coalesce into single outbound at near-intact amounts.

evidence.label: All inbound counterparties are brand-new, single-contact
evidence.value: 15 distinct inbound senders, each with exactly 1 transaction and identical first/last contact timestamps. No repeating relationships. Examples: 8048E30F0 (1 txn, 2022-09-05 11:20), 80A2DE560 (1 txn, 2022-09-04 18:24), 80028E6D0 (1 txn, 2022-09-04 17:03). Classic mule behavior.

evidence.label: Upstream senders are themselves mule-shaped
evidence.value: 8048E30F0: spray_ratio=1.0, collect_ratio=0.5, new_sender_share_24h=1.0 (all new). 80028E6D0: spray_ratio=1.8, passthrough_ratio=0.001, distinct_receivers=9. Both are high-spraying hubs feeding single-contact payments into subject, confirming organized ring.

evidence.label: GATHER-SCATTER structure confirmed by trace_funds
evidence.value: Inbound trace shows FAN-IN: 15 hop-1 senders funnel into 8010D4440. Outbound trace shows subject forwards to 2 main receivers (80CD41030, 8021F0F10), both of which are hubs (collect_ratio=1.0–1.33, spray_ratio=0.5–0.67) with new counterparties. Downstream receivers 8021F0F10 sends $3.59M EUR to 8028DCB80, suggesting final cash-out or FX egress.

evidence.label: New account, intensive activity, generic shell entity
evidence.value: Account first seen 2022-09-02 23:18, only 8 days old at alert. Registered to 'Sole Proprietorship #3911' with 9 sibling accounts under same entity (8010D4440, 80C0AFF60, 80AE6E630, 801A9A2A0, 8102A2E00, 8064F40A0, 801988B80, 808E33340, 80D419D80, 805CCE0B0). Generic name and sibling structure suggest temporary shell network for operation.

evidence.label: Internal conversion and layering
evidence.value: Transaction 1854513 shows self-transfer USD→EUR (2321.52 USD → 1981.18 EUR on 2022-09-06 12:20). Immediately followed by forward to 8021F0F10 (same amount in EUR, same timestamp). Suggests deliberate currency conversion to obscure fund flow.

evidence.label: Amounts are varied, not mechanical
evidence.value: Inbound amounts range from $134 to $17,498; outbound from $1,981 EUR to $18,721 USD. Varied amounts reduce suspicion of automated mule operation, but are consistent with a curated network of sources feeding a consolidation account.

evidence.label: Multi-currency handling
evidence.value: Subject transacts in USD, EUR, CHF, Yuan. While consistent with legitimate FX or remittance services, combined with one-hit counterparties and rapid pass-through, it indicates layering and obfuscation rather than core FX business.

evidence.label: No repeating customer or supplier base
evidence.value: Unlike a real business (merchant, FX desk, payment processor), subject has zero repeating inbound senders and zero repeating outbound receivers. This is inconsistent with any sustainable commerce operation and consistent with temporary mule network.

evidence.label: No prior alerts
evidence.value: Account is only 8 days old and this is the first alert, consistent with a newly-spun-up mule network that operates briefly before shutdown.

case.summary: Account 8006CC9A0 received a single $4.7M inbound transfer on Sept 1 and subsequently scattered funds across 17 distinct receivers over 7 days, totaling $733k outbound. The primary recipient (8006D62F0) forwards 98% of its intake to a downstream account (808B3D530) that exhibits perfect passthrough behavior (passthrough_ratio=1.0) and high fan-in collection (collect_ratio=2.5)—classic mule signatures. The subject itself shows rapid dispersion to many small, unrelated counterparties with repeating micro-payment patterns. However, most outbound counterparties are established (not brand-new), and the subject is registered as a legitimate corporation with no prior alerts. The traced 2-hop structure aligns with SCATTER-GATHER laundering typology, but ambiguity remains whether the subject is a knowingly complicit node or an unwitting gateway account distributing legitimate corporate funds that were subsequently laundered downstream.

evidence.label: Multi-hop traced structure confirmed
evidence.value: Inbound: 8006C8B80 ($4.7M) → 8006CC9A0 → Outbound to 8006D62F0 ($533k) → 808B3D530 (mule collector). 808B3D530 shows passthrough_ratio=1.0 and collect_ratio=2.5, indicating pure-forward behavior characteristic of layering in laundering chains.

evidence.label: High-volume rapid dispersion pattern
evidence.value: Subject dispersed $4.7M inbound across 100 outbound transactions to 17 distinct receivers within 7 days, with heaviest concentration on alert day (14 txns, $34.9k outbound).

evidence.label: Repeating micro-payment cadence
evidence.value: Daily recurring payments to counterparty 800B72820: $6,556 (Cheque) + $4,408 (Credit Card) patterns repeated Sept 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7. Atypical for business operations; consistent with card refund mule or structured layering.

evidence.label: New account with one-time large inbound
evidence.value: Account first seen Sept 1; received single $4.7M inbound transfer on first day. Subsequently only 1 inbound transaction in 9-day window. Pattern inconsistent with ongoing business operations.

evidence.label: Established counterparty relationships contradict all-new flush
evidence.value: Top outbound counterparty 800B72820 has 14 historical transactions with first contact Sept 1, establishing repeating relationship. Other receivers (8079F6490, 800A6F8F0) similarly show multi-transaction history dating to Sept 1. Not indicative of one-off mule dispersion.

evidence.label: Subject's passthrough ratio = 0.0
evidence.value: Per alert features, subject does not operate as pure pass-through for any single sender-receiver pair. Instead, it MIXES and SCATTERS inbound across multiple recipients. Real pass-through mules show high passthrough ratios (0.8+); subject shows 0.0.

evidence.label: Legitimate entity registration
evidence.value: Subject is registered as 'Corporation #822' at National Bank of Laramie; has 6 sibling accounts within same entity. Not a phantom or shell account; entity maintains multiple accounts suggesting operational structure.

evidence.label: No prior alerts or escalations
evidence.value: First alert on account; no prior risk flags in system. If subject were a known mule in active ring, would expect pattern of earlier alerts or related account flags.

evidence.label: Downstream mule confirmed via account features
evidence.value: Account 808B3D530 (2-hop recipient from subject) exhibits passthrough_ratio=1.0, collect_ratio=2.5, distinct_receivers=1, distinct_senders=5. This is textbook mule signature (collects from many, forwards to one at 1:1 ratio).

evidence.label: Mixed payment formats and bilateral activity
evidence.value: Subject uses Cheque, Credit Card, ACH formats; shows both inbound (1 txn) and outbound (100 txns). Legitimate business indicator. However, inbound is single large lump, not recurring.

case.summary: Account 803FF70C0 is a new collector account (opened Sept 2, 2022) with inflows from six brand-new counterparties totaling $11.6K, but zero outbound activity. While the account sits within a suspicious upstream structure featuring a massive sprayer (80047FAF0, $372M distributed) feeding through mule-shaped intermediaries, the subject itself exhibits no pass-through behavior and does not actively relay funds. The account is dormant, not an active conduit; it collects but does not disperse. No clear laundering typology applies to the subject alone, though upstream entities show mule characteristics.

evidence.label: All-new counterparties, one-shot inflows
evidence.value: Six distinct senders, each with single transaction dated Sept 2–6, 2022 (within account creation window). No repeating relationships. New_sender_share = 1.0.

evidence.label: Pure collector account with zero outflows
evidence.value: Account history shows 7 inbound transactions, 0 outbound. No pass-through detected across 48-hour window. Contradicts alert feature flags (n_out_7d: 7) — data inconsistency.

evidence.label: Upstream GATHER-SCATTER structure
evidence.value: Trace_funds reveals 80047FAF0 (sprayer: $372M outbound, 11 receivers) → hop-1 intermediaries (803FF5A10, 803FF5200, 803FF53E0: collect_ratio 1.0–2.0, spray_ratio 0.67–0.75) → subject (final collector). Classic layering shape.

evidence.label: Hop-1 intermediaries show mule characteristics
evidence.value: Accounts 803FF5A10, 803FF5200, 803FF53E0 have: collect_ratio > 1.0, spray_ratio 0.67+, passthrough ~0, new_receiver_share 30–50%. Pattern consistent with mixing and redistribution nodes.

evidence.label: New entity with no operational footprint
evidence.value: Corporation #10075 created Sept 2, 2022. No established business activity, repeating vendors, or operational payments. Sibling accounts exist but no interaction. Profile inconsistent with legitimate business.

evidence.label: No pass-through behavior (Q2 = NO)
evidence.value: Subject retains all inflows; money does not exit within hours at same ratio. Real mules forward ~100% of inflows intact; this account keeps everything. Contradicts active laundering hypothesis.

evidence.label: Shared counterparty with sprayer
evidence.value: Subject and 80047FAF0 both transact with 803FF5A10. Indicates shared network, but not a closed ring or cycle.

evidence.label: No prior alerts on subject
evidence.value: First and only alert on this account. No pattern of past suspicious activity.

case.summary: Account 80FD2DBC0 received a large inbound (791.6M Shekels on Sept 1) and dispersed funds to 29 receivers at a high rate, triggering FAN_OUT_SPRAY and HIGH_VELOCITY rules. However, the subject itself retains 97% of inbound funds and does not pass money through at high ratios (passthrough_ratio=0.0). Outbound recipients are repeat counterparties established 4-9 days prior, not brand-new contacts. The account behaves like a legitimate business hub (e.g., payroll, distribution, or merchant aggregator) rather than a mule. Downstream receivers show higher pass-through characteristics, but the subject's own pattern—retention, mixing, and repeated relationships—is inconsistent with knowing money laundering. No completed laundering structure detected.

evidence.label: Inbound concentration
evidence.value: Single large inbound (791.6M Shekels) on Sept 1; only 1 distinct sender.

evidence.label: Outbound spray
evidence.value: 29 distinct receivers over 5 days; fan_out=26, spray_ratio=13, 27 txns on Sept 5 alone.

evidence.label: Downstream passthrough risk
evidence.value: Immediate receivers 80FE1CA20 (passthrough=0.92) and 80FD2F530 (passthrough=0.65) show high pass-through characteristics.

evidence.label: Subject passthrough ratio
evidence.value: passthrough_ratio=0.0; subject retains inbound and does not forward ~100% intact within hours.

evidence.label: Money retention
evidence.value: Outbound ~22M vs inbound ~791.6M over 5 days (2.8% outflow); account accumulates, not pipes.

evidence.label: Established counterparties
evidence.value: Outbound receivers are repeating contacts (4–9 days old); no brand-new mule network.

evidence.label: Entity and formats
evidence.value: Real entity (Sole Proprietorship #37510, National Bank of Pittsburgh); varied formats (Cheque, Credit Card); diverse receiver sizes.

evidence.label: No cycle or stack structure
evidence.value: Trace-in/out show inbound hub dispersing to many, no cycles back to root or tight chains.

evidence.label: No prior alerts
evidence.value: get_prior_alerts returned empty; no prior risk signals on this account.

evidence.label: Outbound timing and amounts
evidence.value: Dispersal spread over 5 days with varied amounts (50 Shekels to 6.3M), not time-pressured mule behavior.

case.summary: Account 811A64F10 was flagged for FAN_OUT_SPRAY and STRUCTURING. Investigation shows the subject is a distributor account within a large registered corporation (390+ sibling accounts) that received one large payment from a separate Saudi partnership and distributed ~1.1% onward to 16 receivers over several days. The account does NOT exhibit pass-through behavior (retains ~98.9% of funds) and maintains established, repeating relationships with its counterparties. While some downstream recipients show mule-like metrics (100% new counterparties, high spray ratios), the subject itself is a legitimate business hub, not a pass-through mule.

evidence.label: Account profile & entity
evidence.value: Registered as Corporation #36539 with 390+ sibling accounts; operates via Israel Bank #6; first seen 2022-09-01, active 5+ days with 84 outbound transactions

evidence.label: Money retention (Q2)
evidence.value: Received 48.17M SAR on 2022-09-01; distributed ~550K SAR total over 7 days. Retained ~98.9% internally. No high-ratio pass-through pairs (all ratios <0.3%).

evidence.label: Counterparty relationship freshness (Q1)
evidence.value: Outbound counterparties show repeating relationships: 811C180A0 (8 txns over 3 days), 811B69E60 (6 txns over 4 days), 811DADF00 (4 txns over 1 day), 811D666E0 (6 txns over 4 days). Not brand-new relationships.

evidence.label: Network structure (Q3 partial)
evidence.value: Traced as GATHER-SCATTER: sources feed into 811A64E70 (separate entity), which forwards to subject, which distributes to 16 receivers. Subject is downstream distributor, not upstream collector of ill-gotten funds.

evidence.label: Downstream account risk
evidence.value: Primary recipients (811DADF00, 811A685E0, 811C180A0) show suspicious metrics: 100% new senders/receivers in 24h windows, high spray/passthrough ratios (1.0-2.25). These accounts appear to be mule-shaped.

evidence.label: Payment formats & diversification
evidence.value: Transactions use ACH, Cheque, Credit Card—diverse professional formats. Mix of currencies (Saudi Riyal, Shekel) and amounts (52 SAR to 155K SAR), consistent with business operations.

evidence.label: Inbound source verification
evidence.value: Single inbound source 811A64E70 is part of separate entity (Partnership #6553, Saudi Arabia Bank #6), not controlled by subject's entity. Funds sourced from different organization.

evidence.label: No prior alerts
evidence.value: No prior alerts on this account despite high activity flag suggests this is first suspicious signal, not pattern.

AL-03732458.summary: Account 811AF2F80 exhibits clear signs of money laundering via a GATHER-SCATTER pass-through structure. The subject receives funds from upstream mule 80D41E7A0, immediately forwards them to distribution hub 800728800 at near-identity amounts within hours, with zero lag on some transactions. All counterparties are brand-new (zero repeats); the entity is a vague partnership established 9 days ago with minimal transaction history; and the account shows a passthrough_ratio of 0.945, indicating almost all inbound money exits unchanged. Trace analysis confirms the subject sits in a linear chain between upstream and downstream mule accounts, both exhibiting classic mule features (high passthrough and spray ratios, all-new relationships). This pattern is inconsistent with any legitimate business.

evidence.label: Pass-through pairs with near-identity ratios and short lags
evidence.value: 80D41E7A0 → 811AF2F80 → 811AF2F80: ratio 0.984, lag 2.72 hrs; 811AF2F80 → 800728800: ratio 1.0, lag 0 hrs; passthrough_ratio on subject is 0.945

evidence.label: All active counterparties are brand-new
evidence.value: Inbound: 80D41E7A0 (first contact 2022-09-10 13:04, 1 txn), 811AF2FD0 (first contact 2022-09-09 20:02, 1 txn); Outbound: 800728800 (first contact 2022-09-10 13:04, 1 txn). Zero repeating relationships.

evidence.label: Embedded in a linear chain with mule-shaped neighbors
evidence.value: Upstream 80D41E7A0: passthrough_ratio 0.948, spray_ratio 0.667, new_sender_share_24h 0.5. Downstream 800728800: spray_ratio 1.667, collects from multiple sources and distributes to multiple final recipients. Subject is in the middle forwarding funds unchanged.

evidence.label: Account behaves as a pass-through, not a business
evidence.value: Only 3 outbound and 5 inbound transactions in 9 days; all one-off relationships; tiny amounts (max €12.7K, $14.6K); immediate forwarding of received funds; no fund retention or mixing

evidence.label: Entity profile inconsistent with legitimate business
evidence.value: Generic entity name ('Partnership #25291'); account only 9 days old; part of a multi-account entity structure (3 sibling accounts); no repeating counterparties; trivial transaction volume; mixed payment formats including unusual 'Reinvestment' label

evidence.label: Trace shows classic gather-scatter money flow
evidence.value: Upstream source → 80D41E7A0 → 811AF2F80 (subject) → 800728800 (hub) → 800A78120 and 8068532B0 (final recipients). Each hop preserves amount and adds only minor lags.

AL-04598260.summary: Subject account 8035ABA40 exhibits strong structural indicators of money laundering within a multi-hop ring. The account collected 523,329 Yuan from 24 brand-new counterparties in a burst of 18 transactions within 23 minutes on 2022-09-09. All inbound counterparties are first-contact relationships, and detailed analysis of hop-1 accounts (8035A7480, 8035A6650, 8035B1720, 8035B0A10) reveals mule-like features: spray ratios of 1.0–1.5, passthrough ratios=0, and critically, 100% brand-new senders (new_sender_share_24h=1.0), indicating they are intermediate collection points in a larger ring. The subject account is 9 days old and owned by a sole proprietorship with 21 sibling accounts. However, as of the alert timestamp, no high-ratio short-lag outbound dispersal of the inbound funds has occurred; the only prior outbound transactions (2 on 2022-09-01) predate the inbound burst. Escalation is warranted to monitor for imminent outbound scatter that would confirm the GATHER-SCATTER structure and trigger definitive action.

evidence.label: All 24 inbound counterparties are brand-new (first contact 2022-09-09)
evidence.value: 100% of inbound counterparties have single transaction with first/last timestamps on alert date; n_txns=1 each

evidence.label: Massive fan-in burst within compressed timeframe
evidence.value: 18 of 25 inbound transactions occurred within 23 minutes (13:31–13:54 UTC on 2022-09-09); total 523,329 Yuan collected

evidence.label: Subject account is very young
evidence.value: First seen 2022-09-01; alert on 2022-09-09 (8 days old); activity historically dormant until burst

evidence.label: Hop-1 inbound counterparties exhibit mule characteristics
evidence.value: Sampled accounts (8035A7480, 8035A6650, 8035B1720, 8035B0A10) show spray_ratio=1.0–1.5, passthrough_ratio=0, and new_sender_share_24h=1.0 (100% brand-new senders), indicating they are intermediate mules, not end-users

evidence.label: Subject entity has unusually high number of sibling accounts
evidence.value: Entity 800A26D80 ('Sole Proprietorship #41202') owns 21 other accounts, suggesting a distributed ring structure rather than a single business

evidence.label: One inbound counterparty (8035A66F0) shows perfect pass-through signature
evidence.value: Account features: passthrough_ratio=1.0, collect_ratio=1.0, spray_ratio=0.5 (though data stale as of 2022-09-01)

evidence.label: No outbound dispersal of inbound funds observed as of alert time
evidence.value: Only 2 outbound transactions on record, both on 2022-09-01 (prior to the 2022-09-09 inbound burst); no recent outbound activity correlating to the inbound amounts

evidence.label: Low pass-through ratio for subject account itself
evidence.value: get_pass_through returns only self-transfers; no high-ratio short-lag pairs matching inbound to external outbound recipients

evidence.label: No prior alerts on subject account
evidence.value: get_prior_alerts returned empty; this is the first detection

evidence.label: Trace_funds shows clear FAN-IN structure with mule hop-1 accounts
evidence.value: trace_funds in 2 hops: subject receives from 15 distinct hop-1 accounts (plus self-transfer); hop-1 accounts show secondary activity at hop-2, consistent with multi-layer ring

rule.name: RAPID_COLLECT_FANIN
rule.rationale: Accounts that suddenly collect from many first-time senders in a single day, at collection ratios far above their norm, match the fan-in head of the reported case — FAN_IN_SPIKE misses it because the burst clears in under a day.

missed.context: Victim reports: Yuan 1,574 arrived in 1 transfer(s) from 1 counterpart(ies); US Dollar 219 left in 1 transfer(s) to 1 account(s) over ~3 day(s). No alert was raised at the time.
Victim reports: Euro 15,646 arrived in 1 transfer(s) from 1 counterpart(ies) over ~1 day(s). No alert was raised at the time.
Victim reports: Euro 26,666 arrived in 2 transfer(s) from 2 counterpart(ies); Euro 25,071 left in 2 transfer(s) to 2 account(s) over ~10 day(s). No alert was raised at the time.
Victim reports: US Dollar 11,968,319 arrived in 10 transfer(s) from 10 counterpart(ies) over ~10 day(s). No alert was raised at the time.
Victim reports: Euro 213,603 arrived in 28 transfer(s) from 2 counterpart(ies); Euro 177,182 left in 21 transfer(s) to 2 account(s) over ~12 day(s). No alert was raised at the time.
Victim reports: US Dollar 3,786 arrived in 1 transfer(s) from 1 counterpart(ies) over ~6 day(s). No alert was raised at the time.
Victim reports: Euro 25,542 arrived in 2 transfer(s) from 2 counterpart(ies) over ~8 day(s). No alert was raised at the time.
Victim reports: Australian Dollar 5,710 arrived in 1 transfer(s) from 1 counterpart(ies) over ~1 day(s). No alert was raised at the time.
Victim reports: Yuan 86,374 arrived in 1 transfer(s) from 1 counterpart(ies); US Dollar 11,802 left in 1 transfer(s) to 1 account(s) over ~1 day(s). No alert was raised at the time.
Victim reports: Yuan 20,441 arrived in 1 transfer(s) from 1 counterpart(ies); US Dollar 3,443 left in 1 transfer(s) to 1 account(s) over ~3 day(s). No alert was raised at the time.
Victim reports: US Dollar 7,836 arrived in 1 transfer(s) from 1 counterpart(ies); Canadian Dollar 9,456 left in 1 transfer(s) to 1 account(s) over ~3 day(s). No alert was raised at the time.
Victim reports: Rupee 717,081 arrived in 1 transfer(s) from 1 counterpart(ies) over ~2 day(s). No alert was raised at the time.
Victim reports: US Dollar 9,443 arrived in 1 transfer(s) from 1 counterpart(ies) over ~3 day(s). No alert was raised at the time.
Victim reports: US Dollar 109,883 arrived in 7 transfer(s) from 7 counterpart(ies) over ~3 day(s). No alert was raised at the time.
Victim reports: US Dollar 108,005 arrived in 13 transfer(s) from 13 counterpart(ies); US Dollar 47,307 left in 15 transfer(s) to 15 account(s) over ~7 day(s). No alert was raised at the time.
Victim reports: US Dollar 9,548 arrived in 1 transfer(s) from 1 counterpart(ies) over ~3 day(s). No alert was raised at the time.
Victim reports: US Dollar 11,736 arrived in 1 transfer(s) from 1 counterpart(ies) over ~3 day(s). No alert was raised at the time.
Victim reports: Saudi Riyal 357,340 arrived in 13 transfer(s) from 2 counterpart(ies); Saudi Riyal 317,768 left in 10 transfer(s) to 2 account(s) over ~10 day(s). No alert was raised at the time.

error_patterns: Single exculpatory heuristics (retention ratio, repeat counterparties) allowed to override structural evidence at extreme volumes — caused the missed mule CS-02533989.
First-hop shape read as proof: a fan-in of brand-new senders treated as laundering without second-hop confirmation — caused the false alarm CS-01854515.
Overconfident terminal actions where escalate was available: approve at 0.72–0.78 and block at 0.92 on evidence the write-up itself called two-sided.

cards.text: High retention and repeat counterparties do NOT clear an account that received one anomalous massive inbound and sprays to many receivers — trace the receivers, and if any show pass-through shape, escalate.

Account newness plus all-new fan-in senders is a two-sided signal (mule *or* young merchant). Without confirmed structure at the second hop — a cycle, a stack, or downstream mule shape — cap the action at escalate; never block above 0.8 on first-hop shape alone.
When the alerted flow is ≥100× the account's historical average, do not auto-approve regardless of mechanics — file as escalate and let the analyst clear it.

review.summary: All three disagreements are calibration failures, not evidence failures: the investigations surfaced the right facts, but the final action ignored how two-sided the evidence was. The corrections shift the decision boundary toward **escalate** whenever structural evidence conflicts with exculpatory heuristics, and reserve approve/block for cases where both hops of the money trail agree.

reflections.label: mule
reflections.post_mortem: I cleared 80FD2DBC0 because it retained 97% of inbound funds and paid repeat counterparties — I treated retention as decisive. The analyst's label says this account is the head of a distribution structure: a single 791.6M inbound on day one followed by a 29-receiver spray at high velocity is the gather leg of GATHER-SCATTER, and my own evidence flagged the downstream receivers as higher pass-through. Retention at the first hop doesn't clear an account when the structure continues below it — I should have traced one more hop and escalated instead of approving at 0.78.

reflections.label: normal
reflections.post_mortem: I blocked 8010D4440 at 0.92 on a textbook GATHER-SCATTER read: 15 brand-new single-contact senders, high-ratio forwarding at 17–43 hour lags, an 8-day-old entity with a generic shell name. The analyst judged it a newly onboarded merchant collecting customer payments and sweeping revenue onward — every signal I used is also the shape of a young business. My mistake was certainty: with no cycle, no stack, and no confirmed mule shape at the second hop, newness plus fan-in supports escalate, not a 0.92 block.

reflections.label: suspicious
reflections.post_mortem: I approved 811A64F10 as corporate treasury: a 48.2M inflow fragmented into 84 small payments to 11 repeat recipients, with no high-ratio pass-through. The mechanics genuinely look benign, but the scale was not mine to clear — a single inflow three orders of magnitude above the account's own average deserves a human decision even when every ratio checks out. Approve at 0.72 was over-reach; suspicious was the calibrated call.

rule.name: PASSTHROUGH_SPRAY
rule.rationale: The reported account forwarded nearly everything it received within hours while spraying to 6+ receivers — high pass-through plus fan-out on a fresh inflow is the shape the victim described, and no built-in rule combines all three.
